sections.label: Section 610
sections.original: χρηστοὺς νομίζειν οὔτε τοὺς χρηστοὺς κακούς .
sections.translation: or good men bad . I think that casting off a true friend is for a man like casting away the life in his own bosom , which he most loves . You will surely learn about these affairs in time , since time alone reveals a just man .

sections.label: Section 615
sections.original: κακὸν δὲ κἂν ἐν ἡμέρᾳ γνοίης μιᾷ .
sections.translation: But you can discern a bad man even in one day alone .

sections.label: Section 616
sections.original: καλῶς ἔλεξεν εὐλαβουμένῳ πεσεῖν ,
sections.translation: He has spoken well , my king , for one who is taking care not to fall : those who are quick in counsel are not sure .

sections.label: Section 618
sections.original: ὅταν ταχύς τις οὑπιβουλεύων λάθρᾳ
sections.translation: When the stealthy plotter is moving on me quickly , I , too , must be quick in my counterplot .

sections.label: Section 620
sections.original: εἰ δʼ ἡσυχάζων προσμενῶ , τὰ τοῦδε μὲν
sections.translation: If I await him in repose , his ends will have been gained , and mine lost .

sections.label: Section 622
sections.original: τί δῆτα χρῄζεις ; ἦ με γῆς ἔξω βαλεῖν ;
sections.translation: What do you want then ? To banish me from the land ?

sections.label: Section 623
sections.original: ἥκιστα · θνῄσκειν , οὐ φυγεῖν σε βούλομαι .
sections.translation: Hardly . I desire your death , not your exile , so that I might show what a thing envy is .

sections.label: Section 625
sections.original: ὡς οὐχ ὑπείξων οὐδὲ πιστεύσων λέγεις ;
sections.translation: Are you resolved not to yield or believe ?

sections.label: Section 625a
sections.translation: No , for you persuade me that you are unworthy of trust .

sections.label: Section 626
sections.original: οὐ γὰρ φρονοῦντά σʼ εὖ βλέπω .
sections.translation: No , for I see you are not sane .

sections.label: Section 626a
sections.original: τὸ γοῦν ἐμόν .
sections.translation: Sane , at least , in my own interest .

sections.label: Section 627
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐξ ἴσου δεῖ κἀμόν .
sections.translation: But you should be so in mine also .

sections.label: Section 627a
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἔφυς κακός .
sections.translation: You are false .

sections.label: Section 628
sections.original: εἰ δὲ ξυνίης μηδέν ;
sections.translation: But if you understand nothing ?

sections.label: Section 628a
sections.original: ἀρκτέον γʼ ὅμως .
sections.translation: Still I must rule .

sections.label: Section 629
sections.original: οὔτοι κακῶς γʼ ἄρχοντος .
sections.translation: Not if you rule badly .

sections.label: Section 629a
sections.original: ὦ πόλις πόλις .
sections.translation: Hear him , city of Thebes !

sections.label: Section 630
sections.original: κἀμοὶ πόλεως μέτεστιν , οὐχί σοι μόνῳ .
sections.translation: The city is mine too , not yours alone .

sections.label: Section 631
sections.original: παύσασθʼ , ἄνακτες · καιρίαν δʼ ὑμῖν ὁρῶ
sections.translation: Cease , lords . In good time I see Iocasta coming from the house , with whose help you should resolve your present feud .

sections.label: Section 634
sections.original: τί τὴν ἄβουλον , ὦ ταλαίπωροι , στάσιν
sections.translation: Misguided men , why have you raised

sections.label: Section 635
sections.original: γλώσσης ἐπήρασθʼ οὐδʼ ἐπαισχύνεσθε γῆς
sections.translation: such a foolish argument ? Are you not ashamed , while the land is so sick , to stir up troubles of your own ? Come , go into the house — and you , Creon , go to yours — and stop making so much of a petty grief .

sections.label: Section 639
sections.original: ὅμαιμε , δεινά μʼ Οἰδίπους ὁ σὸς πόσις
sections.translation: Kinswoman , Oedipus , your husband ,

sections.label: Section 640
sections.original: δρᾶσαι δικαιοῖ δυοῖν ἀποκρίνας κακοῖν
sections.translation: wants to do one of two terrible things to me , either to thrust me from the land of my fathers or to arrest and slay me .

sections.label: Section 642
sections.original: ξύμφημι · δρῶντα γάρ νιν , ὦ γύναι , κακῶς
sections.translation: Yes indeed , for I have caught him , lady , working evil against my person with his wicked craft .

sections.label: Section 644
sections.original: μή νυν ὀναίμην , ἀλλʼ ἀραῖος , εἴ σέ τι
sections.translation: May I derive no benefit ,

sections.label: Section 645
sections.original: δέδρακʼ , ὀλοίμην , ὧν ἐπαιτιᾷ με δρᾶν .
sections.translation: but perish accursed , if I have done any of the things of which you charge me .

sections.label: Section 646
sections.original: ὦ πρὸς θεῶν πίστευσον , Οἰδίπους , τάδε ,
sections.translation: In the name of the gods , believe it , Oedipus , first for the sake of this awful oath to the gods , then for my sake and for the sake of those who stand before you .

sections.label: Section 1.649
sections.original: πιθοῦ θελήσας φρονήσας τʼ , ἄναξ , λίσσομαι .
sections.translation: Consent , reflect , listen , my king , I beg you .

sections.label: Section 1.650
sections.original: τί σοι θέλεις δῆτʼ εἰκάθω ;
sections.translation: What would you have me grant you ?

sections.label: Section 1.651
sections.original: τὸν οὔτε πρὶν νήπιον νῦν τʼ ἐν ὅρκῳ μέγαν καταίδεσαι .
sections.translation: Respect him who was in the past not foolish , and who now is strong in his oath .

sections.label: Section 1.653
sections.original: οἶδα .
sections.translation: Do you understand what you crave ?

sections.label: Section 1.654
sections.original: φράζε δὴ τί φής .
sections.translation: I do .

sections.label: Section 1.655
sections.original: τὸν ἐναγῆ φίλον μήποτʼ ἐν αἰτίᾳ
sections.translation: Tell me , then , what you mean .

sections.label: Section 1.656
sections.original: σὺν ἀφανεῖ λόγῳ σʼ ἄτιμον βαλεῖν .
sections.translation: That you should never use an unproved rumor to cast a dishonoring charge on the friend who has bound himself with a curse .

sections.label: Section 1.658
sections.original: ζητῶν ὄλεθρον ἢ φυγὴν ἐκ τῆσδε γῆς .
sections.translation: Then be quite aware that when you seek this you are seeking death or exile from this land for me .

sections.label: Section 2.660
sections.original: οὐ τὸν πάντων θεῶν θεὸν πρόμον
sections.translation: No , by the god that stands at the head of all the host of the gods , no , by the sun . Unblest , unbefriended , may I die the worst possible death , if I have this thought !

sections.label: Section 2.665
sections.original: ἀλλά μοι δυσμόρῳ γᾶ φθινὰς
sections.translation: But my unhappy soul is worn by the withering of the land , as well as by the thought that our old sorrows should be crowned by new ones arising from the two of you .

sections.label: Section 2.670
sections.original: ἢ γῆς ἄτιμον τῆσδʼ ἀπωσθῆναι βίᾳ .
sections.translation: or thrust dishonored from the land . Your words , not his , move me to compassion ; but he , wherever he be , shall be hated .

sections.label: Section 2.673
sections.original: στυγνὸς μὲν εἴκων δῆλος εἶ , βαρὺς δʼ , ὅταν
sections.translation: You are truly sullen in yielding , as you are vehement in the excesses of your wrath . But such natures are

sections.label: Section 2.675
sections.original: αὑταῖς δικαίως εἰσὶν ἄλγισται φέρειν .
sections.translation: justly most difficult for themselves to bear .

sections.label: Section 2.676
sections.original: οὔκουν μʼ ἐάσεις κἀκτὸς εἶ ;
sections.translation: Will you not be gone and leave me in peace ?

sections.label: Section 2.678
sections.original: σοῦ μὲν τυχὼν ἀγνῶτος , ἐν δὲ τοῖσδʼ ἴσος .
sections.translation: Lady , why do you hesitate to take this man into the house ?

sections.label: Section 1.680
sections.original: μαθοῦσά γʼ ἥτις ἡ τύχη .
sections.translation: I will , when I have learned what has happened .

sections.label: Section 1.681
sections.original: δόκησις ἀγνὼς λόγων ἦλθε , δάπτει δὲ καὶ τὸ μὴ ʼνʼδικον .
sections.translation: Blind suspicion , bred of talk , arose , and injustice inflicts wounds .

sections.label: Section 1.682
sections.original: ἀμφοῖν ἀπʼ αὐτοῖν ;
sections.translation: On both sides ?

sections.label: Section 1.683
sections.original: ναίχι .
sections.translation: Yes .

sections.label: Section 1.684
sections.original: καὶ τίς ἦν λόγος ;
sections.translation: And what was the story ?

sections.label: Section 1.685
sections.original: ἅλις ἔμοιγʼ , ἅλις , γᾶς προπονουμένας ,
sections.translation: It is enough , I think , enough , when our land is already vexed , that the matter should rest where it stopped .

sections.label: Section 1.687
sections.original: ὁρᾷς ἵνʼ ἥκεις , ἀγαθὸς ὢν γνώμην ἀνήρ ,
sections.translation: Do you see to what you have come , for all your noble intent , in seeking to slacken and blunt my zeal ?

sections.label: Section 2.690
sections.original: ὦναξ , εἶπον μὲν οὐχ ἅπαξ μόνον ,
sections.translation: be sure that I would have proved myself a madman , bankrupt in sane counsel , if I forsook you — you , who gave a true course to my beloved country when it was

sections.label: Section 2.695
sections.original: ἀλύουσαν κατʼ ὀρθὸν οὔρισας ,
sections.translation: distraught with troubles , and who now are likely to prove our prospering guide .

sections.label: Section 2.698
sections.original: μῆνιν τοσήνδε πράγματος στήσας ἔχεις .
sections.translation: In the name of the gods , tell me , king , the reason that you have conceived this steadfast wrath .

sections.label: Section 700
sections.original: ἐρῶ · σὲ γὰρ τῶνδʼ ἐς πλέον , γύναι , σέβω ·
sections.translation: That I will do , for I honor you , lady , above these men . Creon is the cause , and the plots he has laid against me .

sections.label: Section 702
sections.original: λέγʼ , εἰ σαφῶς τὸ νεῖκος ἐγκαλῶν ἐρεῖς .
sections.translation: Come , tell me how the argument began .

sections.label: Section 703
sections.original: φονέα με φησὶ Λαΐου καθεστάναι .
sections.translation: He says that I stand guilty of Laius ’ blood .

sections.label: Section 704
sections.original: αὐτὸς ξυνειδὼς ἢ μαθὼν ἄλλου πάρα ;
sections.translation: On his own knowledge or on hearsay from another ?

sections.label: Section 705
sections.original: μάντιν μὲν οὖν κακοῦργον εἰσπέμψας , ἐπεὶ
sections.translation: He has made a rascal seer his mouth piece : as for himself , he keeps his lips wholly pure .

sections.label: Section 707
sections.original: σύ νυν ἀφεὶς σεαυτὸν ὧν λέγεις πέρι
sections.translation: Then absolve yourself of the things about which you are speaking . Listen to me , and take comfort in learning that nothing of mortal birth shares in the science of the seer .

sections.label: Section 710
sections.original: φανῶ δέ σοι σημεῖα τῶνδε σύντομα .
sections.translation: I will give you a pithy proof of this . An oracle came to Laius once — I will not say from Phoebus himself , but from his ministers — saying that he would suffer his doom at the hands of the child to be born to him and me .

sections.label: Section 715
sections.original: καὶ τὸν μέν , ὥσπερ γʼ ἡ φάτις , ξένοι ποτὲ
sections.translation: And Laius — as , at least , the rumor goes — was murdered one day by foreign robbers at a place where the three highways meet . And the child ’ s birth was not yet three days past , when Laius pinned his ankles together and had him thrown , by others ’ hands , on a remote mountain .
